reason: All 21 FRs have bidirectional traces (FR→SRS→Code→Test). 23 cross-cutting tests per SRS §6. ASPICE SWE.3 SP1/SP2/SP3 all PASS. Internal consistency 100%. Minor: FR-18 lint-only (acceptable per SRS), cross-cutting tests lack unique IDs, NFR reverse trace is type-level not per-ID.

gaps.description: FR-18 (code conventions) has no dedicated code file or unit test — CI lint gate only. Acceptable per SRS but technically breaks the 'every FR has a code file and test file' pattern.
Cross-cutting test cases (23 total) lack unique test IDs (e.g., TC-01..TC-23). Descriptive names are used but make individual tracking in test management tools harder.

NFR→FR reverse mapping is aggregated by NFR type (Performance, Security, etc.) rather than per individual NFR ID (NFR-01, NFR-02...). The SPEC_TRACKING.md reference suggests this exists externally but is not replicated in the matrix itself.
